My name is Cynthia Taylor and today I will be answering the three questions said to me via email regarding my experience with CTE and so I'm going to start with the first question which asked me to tell you about a time or had to build a CTE program. I used to work for Florida Technical College as the program director for criminal justice, IT and cybersecurity. They didn't have a cybersecurity program when I started and so I was responsible for helping to build the program from the ground up to get it ready to go into our catalogs the next year. So I had to find the subject matter experts. I had to deal with the budget. I had to coordinate all of the design of the horses being created that included going through any of the certifications that we're going to be able to be taken after they go through the program. So cybersecurity plus CompTIA A plus and all those different certifications. So I was responsible for that entire coordination process. The next question asked me to answer a time where I had to deal with a program that I may not have known as well, but how did I use my experience to deal with that. So using the same example of working with Florida Technical College, I was the program director of criminal justice and IT and business, but I also oversaw the allied health. So similar to my construct when dealing with programs that I didn't know more closely like IT and finance and business. When I dealt with criminal justice and allied health, I leaned very heavily on subject matter experts. I use a lot of advisory boards. And so I just made sure that they were able to support us in any type of design that we had to do if we had to make any adjustments in our programming. I worked very closely with those individuals. Now I did also with my experience having taught for 20 plus years. You know, I understood how to go and get the standards if I needed to understand how to do the alignment with the programs and still be able to manage those different entities, even though they weren't directly related to my background. When I worked for the University of Phoenix, for example, I was also responsible for our are in registered nursing to bachelor's and nursing programs. And I worked very closely with the hospitals to see what they wanted for individuals that would be graduating from our program. And then we, you know, did a crosswalk of aligning our curriculum with their requirements as well. So just leaning really heavily on subject matter experts when I did not have the personal expertise myself. In regards to the final question on drafting a CTE course and what would I check to ensure that it is done right. So again, with this one is all about alignment, you know, so if I wanted to line up a program, even if I go back to when I also did a HR certification, PHR certification course where this one I actually physically taught it myself. So I had to start with what the certification requirements were. Align what the in the course teachings are with the certification and make sure that all the students had and we actually taught from the certification curriculums. So I infuse that in the 3 month course that I personally build and taught to prepare the students for the PHR certification. So it's really about making sure that you do a gap analysis to. Align your course curriculum with any certification or any industry standards to make sure that you hit all those targets when you're doing your CTE programs. Thank you.
